name: Ye Olde Kings Head
address: 116 Santa Monica Blvd, Santa Monica, CA 90401
about: The oldest British pub in LA since 1974. The definitive home for England supporters on the Westside — expect a packed house, pints of Guinness, and commentary in English for every Three Lions match.

name: Escorpión
about: The undisputed home of Mexico and Latin American football in LA. Every El Tri match fills the bar with a sea of green jerseys, mariachi energy, and Spanish-language commentary at full blast.

name: El Compadre
address: 7408 W Sunset Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90046
about: A Hollywood landmark for Mexican football fans. Flaming cheese, fiery margaritas, and a match-day atmosphere that erupts with every El Tri goal. Commentary in Spanish on the main screens.

name: Red Lion Tavern
about: Silver Lake's beloved German beer hall since 1959. The biergarten transforms into a Bavarian street party when Germany plays. Authentic German beer, bratwurst, and commentary in German on the garden screens.

name: The Stadium Club
about: Koreatown's premier sports bar. Every South Korea match turns this place into a sea of Red Devils. Korean fried chicken, soju cocktails, and Korean-language commentary that makes you feel like you're in Seoul.

name: Café Brasil
address: 11736 W Washington Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90066
about: West LA's Brazilian community hub and the place to be when the Seleção plays. Açaí bowls, caipirinhas, and Portuguese-language commentary with a crowd that dances samba after every goal.

name: Highland Park Bowl
address: 5621 N Figueroa St, Los Angeles, CA 90042
about: A restored 1920s bowling alley that doubles as a football bar. The Portuguese and Brazilian communities have claimed it for World Cup. Craft beer, artisanal pizza, and big screens above the lanes.

name: Casey's Irish Bar
address: 613 S Grand Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90017
about: A Downtown LA institution since 1989. Giant projection screens, a full Guinness menu, and a lively Irish-American atmosphere. The French supporters group has adopted it as their DTLA base.

name: Cock & Bull
about: A proper British pub that takes football seriously. Outdoor patio for overflow crowds, early openings for European kick-offs, and a split allegiance between England and the USA.

name: LALA's Argentine Grill
about: The heart of LA's Argentine community. When the Albiceleste plays, LALA's erupts with chants, flags, and asado smoke. Spanish commentary, empanadas, and Malbec flowing — it's Buenos Aires in Hollywood.

name: Barney's Beanery
address: 8447 Santa Monica Blvd, West Hollywood, CA 90069
about: An LA institution since 1920 with 40+ screens. Pool tables get pushed aside for World Cup season and the full length of the bar hums with energy. A true melting-pot crowd from every nation.

name: Le Petit Paris
address: 418 S Spring St, Los Angeles, CA 90013
about: Downtown's elegant French brasserie becomes the unofficial French consulate on Les Bleus match days. Croissants, croque-monsieurs, and French-language commentary with a stylish Parisian crowd.

name: Big Wangs
about: Hollywood's rowdiest sports bar with zero pretension and maximum atmosphere. Wings, cheap pitchers, and screens absolutely everywhere. World Cup here is a sensory overload in the best way.

name: The Greyhound
about: Silver Lake's laid-back British gastropub with a beautiful back patio. Mixed crowd of expats and local converts, great craft beer, proper fish & chips. A calm haven for watching football outdoors.

name: The Brig
about: Venice Beach's coolest sports bar on iconic Abbot Kinney. Huge outdoor area perfect for warm summer kickoffs, curated beer list, and a design-forward crowd that still goes wild when goals hit.

name: The Association
about: Sleek DTLA sports bar with three giant projection walls. The French and Spanish supporter communities have made it their base. Craft cocktails and a sophisticated late-night vibe.

name: Mercado Buenos Aires
about: The Valley's Argentine stronghold. Mercado Buenos Aires is part deli, part restaurant, and 100% football mad. During World Cup the outdoor patio fills with Argentine flags and the grill never stops.

name: The Fox and Hounds
about: British pub on Main Street that's been hosting England watch parties for decades. Full English breakfast on early kick-off days, proper ales on tap, and a garden patio with an outdoor screen.
